things.name: Old Montreal (Vieux-Montréal)
things.description: 17th-century cobblestoned historic district with Notre-Dame Basilica, the Old Port and horse-drawn calèches

things.name: Notre-Dame Basilica
things.description: Gothic Revival masterpiece illuminated by the AURA light show — the most visited attraction in Quebec

things.name: Mount Royal Park
things.description: Frederick Law Olmsted's 1876 park at the summit of the city — Chalet lookout panorama in summer and winter

things.name: Plateau Mont-Royal
things.description: Montreal's most characterful neighbourhood — spiral staircases, colourful duplexes, bagel bakeries and jazz bars

things.name: Montreal Biodome & Biosphere
things.description: Living ecosystems from the Americas under one roof — tropical forest, Laurentian maple forest and sub-Antarctic rocks

things.name: Jean-Talon Market
things.description: The largest open-air market in North America — Quebec cheeses, maple syrup, artisan charcuterie and cider

things.name: Underground City (RÉSO)
things.description: 32-km network of underground pedestrian tunnels linking malls, metro and hotels — essential in January

things.name: Montreal International Jazz Festival
things.description: The world's largest jazz festival (late June) — 500+ concerts many of them free in the Quartier des Spectacles

things.name: Schwartz's Deli
things.description: Montreal's most legendary smoked meat deli (1928) — the queue is worth every minute

things.name: Quebec City Day Trip
things.description: North America's most European city — walled Old Quebec, Château Frontenac and the Plains of Abraham, 3 hours east
